steps.title: Convert the table to points on f
steps.content:
Do not regress the $g(x)$ values directly, because the question asks about $f(x)$. Since $g(x)=\frac{f(x)}{x+3}$, multiply each table value by $x+3$:
  • $f(-15)=3(-12)=-36$
  • $f(-6)=0(-3)=0$
  • $f(9)=5(12)=60$
So the graph of $y=f(x)$ passes through $(-15,-36)$, $(-6,0)$, and $(9,60)$.

steps.title: Find the linear function f
steps.content: Use two of the converted points, $(-6,0)$ and $(9,60)$. The slope is $$m=\frac{60-0}{9-(-6)}=\frac{60}{15}=4.$$ Since $f(-6)=0$, use $f(x)=4x+b$:$$0=4(-6)+b\Rightarrow b=24$$So $f(x)=4x+24$.

steps.title: Answer the intercept question
steps.content: The $y$-intercept of $y=f(x)$ is where $x=0$, so the point is $(0,24)$. The result matches.
